name: Anchore Enterprise Container Security API
what_it_does: Container image vulnerability scanning and SBOM (Software Bill of Materials) platform — enabling DevSecOps teams to scan container images for OS and language package vulnerabilities, generate SBOMs in SPDX/CycloneDX/Syft formats, enforce policy gates in CI/CD pipelines, and continuously monitor images in production registries, with both open-source CLI tools (Grype for scanning, Syft for SBOM) and Anchore Enterprise for teams.
use_cases: Scanning container images for CVEs via Anchore/Grype in CI/CD pipeline gates
Generating SBOM reports via Syft for software supply chain compliance and NTIA requirements
Enforcing container security policies via Anchore Enterprise policy bundles before deployment
Continuously monitoring running images for newly disclosed vulnerabilities via Anchore Enterprise
Integrating SBOM-based vulnerability scanning via Anchore into Kubernetes admission controllers
not_for: Runtime container security monitoring (use Falco or Sysdig for runtime protection)
IaC security scanning (use Checkov or tfsec for Terraform/Kubernetes manifests)
Source code SAST (use Semgrep or CodeQL for source analysis)
best_when: A DevSecOps team needs container image vulnerability scanning with SBOM generation and policy enforcement in CI/CD pipelines — particularly for compliance with NTIA SBOM requirements or EO 14028.
avoid_when: You need runtime security, IaC scanning, or source code analysis — Anchore focuses on container images and packages.

auth.notes: Anchore Enterprise: HTTP Basic auth with username/password. API token available via /v1/token endpoint. Grype and Syft CLI: no auth (local tools, use Anchore DB). Open-source tools run locally — no API calls needed. Enterprise REST API on self-hosted or Anchore.io SaaS. Webhook signatures for event notifications.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Grype and Syft: fully free open source (Apache 2.0). Anchore Enterprise: paid.
pricing.paid_tiers: Anchore Enterprise: custom pricing (contact sales); estimated $50K+/year for teams
pricing.notes: US company (Santa Barbara, CA). Founded 2016. $40M+ funding (Series B 2021). Open-source tools: Grype (vulnerability scanner, Apache 2.0) and Syft (SBOM generator, Apache 2.0) — widely adopted. Anchore Enterprise adds: policy engine, continuous monitoring, registry integrations, RBAC, compliance reporting. NTIA SBOM compliance focus. EO 14028 supply chain security alignment. Acquired by DNAnexus? (unconfirmed — check current ownership). Supported formats: Docker, OCI, SBOM (SPDX, CycloneDX, Syft JSON). Vulnerability DB: updated every 5 hours.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted Enterprise — no limits; Anchore.io SaaS: depends on plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Enterprise REST API + CLI tools (Grype, Syft). Apache 2.0 open source. HTTP Basic auth. Webhooks. Self-hosted or SaaS. SBOM and vulnerability scanning. DevSecOps focus. Enterprise pricing opaque.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image analysis: idempotent by image digest. Policy evaluation: deterministic for same image/policy. Webhook delivery: at-least-once — implement idempotent handlers.
known_agent_gotchas: Two products: open-source Grype/Syft CLI (no API) vs Anchore Enterprise (REST API)
Grype/Syft are local tools — no network API calls needed, just run the binary
Image analysis is async — poll /v1/images/{digest}/vuln for results after submission
Policy bundles are complex JSON — use existing templates rather than hand-writing
Vulnerability DB must be updated locally for Grype: grype db update
Enterprise requires self-hosting or Anchore.io — no free cloud tier for API access

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. HTTP Basic auth for Enterprise API (no OAuth2). Apache 2.0 open source — auditable code. SBOM generation for supply chain transparency. Vulnerability DB updated every 5 hours. User-controlled data residency (self-hosted). Container image data sensitivity (may contain app secrets in layers). FedRAMP-ready for government use.
